label: Full-bleed Hero
description: Cinematic, immersive landing page where the hero section spans the full viewport height with a layered background (image, gradient mesh, or subtle motion canvas) and a centered CTA stack — for product launches, lifestyle brands, hospitality, and anything that wants visual impact before reading. Breaks the split-hero default so the hero IS the surface, not a two-column billboard.
signatureMotif: single full-viewport hero, layered background imagery, centered breathing CTA cluster — no two-column split above fold
promptHints: Build the hero as a single full-bleed section spanning the full viewport height (min-h-screen). The background carries the visual story (atmospheric image, gradient mesh, or subtle motion canvas); the foreground holds ONLY a tight centered cluster: eyebrow line, oversized headline (clamp-based, not fixed px), short standfirst, and one or two CTAs.
Do NOT split the hero into a left text column + right product/screenshot card. The whole viewport IS the hero. Subsequent sections can use grids, but section #1 must be visually unified.

Treat negative space as a feature: never fill the hero edges with logos, badges, or chips. Trust the background and let the centered cluster breathe with generous margin from the top/bottom of the fold.

layouts: Hero is one section with min-h-screen, a full-bleed absolute-positioned background layer (image, gradient mesh, or canvas) covering 100% width and height, and a centered foreground cluster with max-w-3xl: eyebrow, h1 (text-5xl→text-7xl responsive), one short standfirst paragraph, primary CTA + ghost CTA stacked or side-by-side.

Use a scroll indicator (animated chevron or thin progress hint) anchored at the bottom-center of the hero, signaling there is more below — never a sticky chat button or floating widget that competes with the hero focus.

Section #2 starts with a generous top padding (py-24 or more) and uses a single-column long-form structure: lead paragraph, pull quote, and a row of three vertical cards — no recreating a split-hero in section 2.
Feature sections alternate full-bleed accent panels (colored or imagery) with constrained centered content blocks (max-w-4xl) so the rhythm stays cinematic, not card-grid-heavy.
Final CTA section returns to full-viewport height with a single centered headline + one CTA, mirroring the hero's compositional weight (bookend pattern).
motifs: Background uses a layered atmospheric photograph (warm tone) OR a CSS gradient mesh (3+ radial gradients with color-mix on theme tokens) OR a quiet animated canvas (e.g. drifting particles at very low opacity); a 30-50% black-to-transparent overlay ensures foreground text reads.

Foreground typography is large and confident: headline at clamp(2.75rem, 6vw, 5.5rem) with tight leading (1.05), eyebrow in small caps with letter-spacing, body at 18px+ for readability against the layered background.

CTAs are pill-shaped or sharp-rectangular (not glassy) with primary in a bold theme accent and secondary as a transparent outline that picks up the background mood — no drop-shadowed cards on top of the hero.
Page rhythm uses occasional full-bleed accent stripes (colored panels spanning 100vw at intermediate sections) to reinforce the cinematic cadence between content blocks.
antiPatterns: Never split the hero into a left text column + right image/product card — that is the corporate-grid / bold-startup territory. This variant's whole reason for existing is to break that default.
Avoid stacking 4+ logo strips, trust badges, or stat chips inside the hero — those belong below the fold. The hero must stay visually quiet so the background reads.
Do not use small or screenshot-heavy hero images placed inside a card; the background should fill 100vw × 100vh, not be a contained graphic.
Never use a generic SaaS gradient (purple-to-blue diagonal) — pick atmospheric, tonal palettes that look photographic or filmic, not template-y.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: radial-gradient(circle at top left, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 12%, transparent) 0%, transparent 38%), radial-gradient(circle at bottom right, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-accent) 10%, transparent) 0%, transparent 36%)
